response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web and backend developer, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his background is almost entirely in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), with no evidence of embedded networking, LAN-switch development, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel internals. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a single short role at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, neither of which demonstrates the depth required for embedded firmware security work.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating strong general engineering ability
Exposure to embedded and low-latency systems (DLang async I/O, hardware work at Altom, C/C++ at National Instruments) suggests some relevant foundational instincts
Strong Linux fluency and familiarity with REST APIs, which partially overlap with preferred qualifications
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a listed hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero demonstrable experience in networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, L2/L3 protocols, NETCONF, SNMP)
No Linux kernel internals experience; career is overwhelmingly focused on application-layer web and cloud development, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain knowledge
